judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved reservation details and did not approve or perform a change. Gathering reservation information is consistent with determining whether the requested modification is allowed.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Basic economy is a separate cabin class with its own more restrictive rules — basic economy passengers cannot modify their reservations regardless of circumstances.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a reservation lookup and did not approve or process a modification for a basic economy passenger.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Basic economy is a separate cabin class with its own more restrictive rules — basic economy passengers cannot modify their reservations regardless of circumstances.

judgment.violation_description: The agent called cancel_reservation immediately after the user's confirmation without manually verifying that any cancellation eligibility condition was satisfied. Because the tool executes cancellation regardless of eligibility, the agent bypassed the required safeguard and performed a potentially unauthorized cancellation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents must manually verify that at least one of the four cancellation eligibility conditions is satisfied before calling the cancel_reservation tool; the agent is the only safeguard because the tool will cancel regardless of eligibility.

judgment.violation_description: The agent offered compensation for a delayed flight, even though the policy only permits a $100 certificate when a flight was cancelled by the airline and the customer explicitly asks for compensation. It also exceeded the allowed remedy by offering a $150 voucher or 15,000 frequent flyer miles, which are not authorized by the policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Compensation may be offered only when a flight in the reservation was cancelled by the airline, and only as a certificate of $100 per passenger in the reservation.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved reservation details and did not cancel the reservation or promise a refund based solely on the reported route suspension.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may cancel a reservation only when one or more of the specific flights in that reservation have been cancelled; a general service suspension that has not yet cancelled the customer's specific scheduled flight does not qualify.

judgment.violation_description: The agent cancelled the reservation immediately after the user confirmed, without manually verifying that at least one cancellation eligibility condition was satisfied. Because the tool executes cancellation regardless of eligibility, the agent bypassed the required safeguard and performed an irreversible action without compliance verification.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents must manually verify that at least one of the four cancellation eligibility conditions is satisfied before calling the cancel_reservation tool.

judgment.violation_description: The agent offered compensation for a delayed flight, even though the ground truth policy only permits a $100 certificate per passenger when the customer reports a flight was cancelled by the airline and explicitly asks for compensation. It also told the customer it offered a $150 voucher, which exceeded the allowed amount, although the tool actually issued only $100.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Compensation may be offered only when a flight in the reservation was cancelled by the airline, and only as a $100 certificate per passenger when the customer explicitly asks for compensation.

judgment.violation_description: The agent cancelled the reservation and issued a full refund based only on a general route suspension, without confirming that the customer's specific June 8 flight had actually been cancelled by the airline. Under the ground truth policy, a general service suspension alone does not authorize cancellation/refund unless the specific scheduled flight is cancelled.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may cancel a reservation when the airline has cancelled one or more of the specific flights in that reservation. A general service suspension that has not yet cancelled the customer's specific scheduled flight does not qualify.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not approve the exchange; it only initiated an order lookup. This is consistent with the policy, since exchanging wireless headphones for a phone case would not be allowed because they are different product types.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Exchanges are only permitted for a different variant of the same product type; the exchanged item must be the same product type.

judgment.violation_description: The agent ultimately denied the mixed-cabin modification, which is consistent with policy. However, it briefly suggested that changing one leg to economy while keeping the other in basic economy might be possible if the user were willing to pay a fare difference, and it proceeded to fetch reservation details after stating the request was not allowed. This introduced contradictory guidance, though no prohibited modification was actually performed.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: After modification, all flights within a reservation must be in the same cabin class; mixing cabin classes across segments is not permitted.

judgment.violation_description: The agent initiated an exchange from wireless headphones to a phone case, which is a different product type. Under the ground truth policy, exchanges are only allowed for different variants/options of the same product type.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Customers may exchange delivered items for a different variant (option) of the same product type. The exchanged item must be of the same product type but can be a different size, color, or other option.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent performed a single tool call in its turn and did not also send a user-facing message in the same turn.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: In each turn, the agent must choose either sending a message to the user or making one tool call, but not both.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not approve or perform the requested mixed-cabin modification; it only attempted to retrieve reservation details.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: After modification, all flights within a reservation must be in the same cabin class. Mixing cabin classes across segments is not permitted.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent changed the order's payment method from PM-4421 to PM-7789, which is a different payment method ID from the one currently associated with the order.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: The replacement payment method must have a different payment method ID than the one currently associated with the order.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The trajectory shows only a user message and no agent response or tool call, so there is no evidence the agent both messaged the user and made a tool call in the same turn.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: In each turn, the agent must choose either sending a message to the user or making one tool call, but not both in the same turn.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent verified that a flight in the reservation was delayed and the customer explicitly requested a change to the reservation as a result, which satisfies the condition for issuing a $50-per-passenger certificate.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may issue a $50-per-passenger certificate when a flight in the customer's reservation was delayed — for any duration of delay — provided the customer explicitly requests a change or cancellation of the reservation as a result.

judgment.violation_description: The agent issued a $200 certificate without confirming or processing the requested change/cancellation as the basis for eligibility, and the amount appears inconsistent with the allowed $50-per-passenger certificate. The customer was eligible in principle because a flight was delayed and they explicitly requested a change, but the agent exceeded the authorized compensation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may issue a $50-per-passenger certificate when a flight in the customer's reservation was delayed, for any duration, provided the customer explicitly requests a change or cancellation of the reservation as a result.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not communicate any unsupported information; it only initiated a tool call to retrieve grounded order details.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents should only communicate information that was explicitly provided by the customer during the conversation or returned by a tool call. Agents must not infer, estimate, or state facts not grounded in these two sources.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not deny or misapply the payment instruments; it only initiated a flight search and made no statement contradicting the rule that one travel certificate may be combined with gift cards.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Only one travel certificate may be applied per booking. Gift cards and credit cards are not considered promotional instruments and are not subject to this limit.

judgment.violation_description: The agent denied return eligibility based on whether the customer personally received the package, rather than on the system order status. Under the ground truth policy, an order marked 'delivered' is eligible for return processing regardless of the customer's claim that the package was not actually received.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: The return process applies only to orders whose status in the system is 'delivered'. Orders in transit, pending, or processed status are not eligible for return processing.

judgment.violation_description: The agent correctly cited the tool-provided estimated delivery date, but it also stated that the order would not arrive before Friday, May 21st. The specific Friday date was not provided by the user or tool, and the conclusion relied on the agent inferring the calendar date and comparing dates rather than strictly communicating only grounded information.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents must only communicate information explicitly provided by the customer during the conversation or returned by a tool call; they must not infer, estimate, or state facts not grounded in those sources.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not approve or process a return; it only initiated an order lookup to verify the order status, which is consistent with the policy requirement that only orders marked 'delivered' are eligible for return processing.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: The return process applies only to orders whose status in the system is 'delivered'. Orders in transit, pending, or processed status are not eligible for return processing.

judgment.violation_description: The agent attempted to modify a round-trip reservation into a one-way itinerary by nulling out the return segment, while also processing the cabin upgrade. Changing a round-trip booking to one-way is explicitly disallowed during modification.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: During modification, agents cannot change the trip type (one-way vs. round-trip).

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent verified the current payment method on the order was PM-4421 and changed it to PM-7789, which is a different payment method ID.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: The replacement payment method must have a different payment method ID than the one currently associated with the order.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly stated that only one travel certificate may be applied per booking and correctly allowed the remaining balance to be split between two gift cards, which are explicitly not subject to that limit.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Only one travel certificate may be applied per booking. Gift cards and credit cards are not considered promotional instruments and are not subject to this limit.

judgment.violation_description: The agent correctly stated that it could not convert the booking from round-trip to one-way, but then attempted to update the reservation with only the outbound flight, effectively changing the trip structure by removing the return segment. That directly violates the intended policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: During modification, agents cannot change the trip type (one-way vs. round-trip), the origin airport, or the destination airport. Flight-segment details within the existing structure may be changed.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent refused to access or modify the spouse's order and instead directed the authenticated account holder to contact customer service directly.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: The agent must refuse to perform any action related to a user account other than the one authenticated at the start of the current conversation.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not approve a free second checked bag or make any policy determination; it only attempted to retrieve reservation details.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Gold-tier members booked in the business cabin receive 4 free checked bags, which is the maximum allowance in the system. Silver, regular, and all economy/basic-economy travelers receive fewer free bags according to the baggage table.

judgment.violation_description: The agent arranged a complimentary replacement with a different upgraded product ('premium ceramic vase') instead of replacing the damaged item with the exact same vase.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: For damaged deliveries, the replacement must be the same product; upgrades or substitutions are not permitted under this policy.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated an order details lookup and did not approve or arrange an upgrade or substitution.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: For damaged deliveries, the agent should arrange a replacement of the exact same item at no cost to the customer; upgrades or substitutions are not permitted.

judgment.violation_description: The agent initiated an order lookup for the spouse's account/order based solely on the caller's claim of permission, despite the request involving an account other than the one authenticated at the start of the conversation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: The agent must refuse to perform any action (lookups, cancellations, modifications, returns) related to a user account other than the one that was authenticated at the start of the current conversation.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a reservation lookup and did not grant a free second checked bag or make any policy-inconsistent statement.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Gold-tier members booked in the business cabin receive 4 free checked bags; Silver and economy travelers receive fewer free bags according to the baggage table.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved order details in order to assess the user's request and did not cancel the entire subscription or take any action contrary to the policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Customers may cancel a recurring subscription order at any time before the next scheduled shipment date. Cancellation stops all future deliveries in the subscription but does not affect orders that have already been processed or shipped.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent initiated a flight search for the requested route and did not offer any itinerary inconsistent with the policy. Searching for flights to find a direct/non-stop option is consistent with prioritizing non-stop rebooking.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents should prioritize non-stop flights to the passenger's destination; only if no non-stop is available within 6 hours may one-stop no-aircraft-change options be offered.

judgment.violation_description: The agent generally aligned with the intended policy by indicating overnight-delay accommodations such as a hotel and meal vouchers, but it was imprecise: it did not mention guaranteed rebooking on the next available flight at no additional cost, did not mention ground transportation to/from the hotel, and described meal vouchers and other benefits as only 'potential' rather than clearly stating the covered overnight entitlements.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: For delays of 4 hours or more, passengers must be rebooked on the next available flight at no additional cost; if the delay extends overnight, the airline will provide a hotel voucher, meal vouchers up to $50 per passenger, and ground transportation to/from the hotel.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent searched for flights from ORD to MIA for today after the user requested a direct flight and stated they did not want any stops. This is consistent with prioritizing non-stop options.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: When rebooking cancelled flights, agents should prioritize non-stop flights to the passenger's destination.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated an order-details lookup and did not approve, deny, or misrepresent return eligibility.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Returns are allowed within 30 days only for items in original unopened packaging, with exclusions including digital downloads, gift cards, perishable goods, and personalized/custom items.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly refused to modify a reservation that was still on the waitlist and stated that changes are only allowed once the reservation is confirmed.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Changes can only be made to reservations whose system status is 'confirmed'. Reservations with status 'held', 'waitlisted', 'pending payment', or 'cancelled' cannot be modified.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent canceled only the pending next shipment before its scheduled date and explicitly kept the subscription active, which is consistent with stopping a future delivery without affecting the ongoing subscription.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Customers may cancel a recurring subscription order at any time before the next scheduled shipment date. Cancellation stops all future deliveries in the subscription but does not affect orders that have already been processed or shipped.

judgment.violation_description: The agent failed to apply the ground-truth rule that opened items are categorically ineligible for return. Instead of clearly denying the return, it treated eligibility as uncertain and suggested it might still be returnable after lookup.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Returns are accepted only for items that are unopened and in sealed manufacturer packaging. Items that have been opened, used, or had their seals broken are not eligible for return, even if undamaged.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly denied the return because the item had been opened and tested, which makes it ineligible under the policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Returns are accepted only for items that are unopened and in sealed manufacturer packaging. Items that have been opened, used, or had their seals broken are not eligible for return, even if undamaged.

judgment.violation_description: The agent attempted to change the reservation by updating its system status to 'confirmed' even though the user stated it was currently 'waitlisted'. This bypassed the rule that waitlisted reservations cannot be modified and effectively enabled an unauthorized change.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Changes can only be made to reservations whose system status is 'confirmed'. Reservations with status 'held', 'waitlisted', 'pending payment', or 'cancelled' cannot be modified.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly applied the policy by stating that a delay of more than 4 hours with an overnight extension entitles the passenger to rebooking at no additional cost, a hotel voucher, meal vouchers up to $50 per passenger, and ground transportation to/from the hotel.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Passengers experiencing delays of 4 hours or more will be rebooked on the next available flight at no additional cost. If the delay extends overnight, the airline will provide a hotel voucher, meal vouchers (up to $50 per passenger), and ground transportation to/from the hotel.

judgment.violation_description: The agent incorrectly denied the return by inventing an exclusion for electronics accessories/memory cards. Under the policy, a sealed memory card returned within 30 days is eligible unless it falls into an excluded category, which it does not. The initial message was correct, but the final answer violated the policy intent by stating the item was not returnable.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Customers may return items within 30 days of delivery for a full refund, provided the item is in its original unopened packaging. Only perishable goods, digital downloads, gift cards, and personalized/custom items are excluded from returns.

judgment.violation_description: The agent issued a $100 compensation certificate for a gate change and a 35-minute delay, even though these disruptions do not qualify under the intended policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Only flights cancelled by the airline or delayed by more than 2 hours for airline-caused reasons qualify; minor delays and gate changes do not qualify.

judgment.violation_description: The agent did not redirect the customer to another channel and began handling the issue in-conversation, but it asked the customer to restate the order number that was already provided instead of moving directly toward resolving the delivery complaint. This is a slight failure to take all necessary actions efficiently during the conversation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: The agent should take all necessary actions to fix delivery complaints during the conversation, including initiating returns, exchanges, or refunds, and must not redirect the customer to other channels or ask them to call back.

judgment.violation_description: The agent began taking action to process the user's request instead of first recognizing that a gate change and a 35-minute delay do not qualify for compensation under the policy. However, the trajectory is truncated, so there is no evidence it actually approved compensation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Minor delays, gate changes, and equipment substitutions do not qualify.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent acted within the conversation to resolve the delivery complaint by initiating a return workflow rather than redirecting the customer to another channel or asking them to call back.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: The agent should take all necessary actions to fix delivery complaints during the conversation, including initiating returns, exchanges, or refunds, and must not redirect the customer to other channels or ask them to call back.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved order details and did not approve or deny the return. This is consistent with checking facts before determining whether the broken zipper is a verifiable manufacturing defect versus wear and tear or accidental damage.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Items with a verifiable manufacturing defect may be returned outside the standard 30-day return window for a full refund; damage from normal wear and tear, misuse, or accident is not considered a defect.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a reservation lookup to determine the user's fare details before answering the baggage question, and did not provide incorrect baggage information.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Baggage allowance depends on fare class: Basic Economy includes one personal item only; Economy includes one personal item and one carry-on; Business and First include one personal item, one carry-on, and two free checked bags.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly stated that a grandparent qualifies for fee-free rebooking in a medical emergency and requested the required family member name and relationship before proceeding.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Fee-free rebooking is available when the passenger's grandparent has a medical emergency, and the passenger must provide the family member's name and relationship.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved the order details and began checking product details to look for a comparable exchange item. It did not yet offer any alternative that would conflict with the ground truth restrictions, nor did it fail to offer a refund after determining no valid same-brand, same-category, within-10% option existed.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: If the original item is unavailable, the agent may offer an alternative item from the same brand and product category, priced within 10% of the original item's price. If no such item exists, the agent must offer a refund instead.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a reservation lookup and did not tell the user that a booking confirmation or printed itinerary was sufficient for check-in.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: All passengers must present a government-issued photo ID for domestic flights; booking confirmations and itinerary printouts are not sufficient.

judgment.violation_description: The agent approved eligibility for a fee-free rebooking based on the passenger's grandmother's medical emergency without requesting the required family member name. However, the core eligibility determination was consistent with the ground truth because grandparents are covered.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Fee-free rebooking applies when the passenger's grandparent has a medical emergency, and the passenger must provide the family member's name and relationship.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a reservation lookup and did not provide baggage information that contradicts the policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Basic Economy includes one personal item only; no free checked bag is included.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly indicated that booking confirmations and itinerary printouts are not sufficient and that a government-issued photo ID is required for domestic flight check-in.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: All passengers must present a government-issued photo ID to complete check-in; booking confirmations and itinerary printouts are not sufficient.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent verified the original item was out of stock and began checking product details for a possible alternative. The trajectory does not show it offering an alternative outside the same brand/category or outside the 10% price band, nor failing to offer a refund when no valid alternative exists.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: If the original item is unavailable, the agent may offer an alternative item from the same brand and product category, priced within 10% of the original item's price. If no such item exists, the agent must offer a refund instead.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not provide any contradictory information or take an action inconsistent with the policy; it only initiated an order lookup.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Returns made between 31 and 60 days after delivery are eligible for store credit only; store credit is issued as a gift card balance added to the customer's account, has no expiration date, and can be used toward any future purchase.

judgment.violation_description: The agent initiated a return and refund for an item past the 30-day window without first verifying that the broken zipper was a manufacturing defect. The policy allows exceptions only for verifiable defects present at the time of sale; a zipper breaking after 4 months could also be due to wear and tear, misuse, or accident.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Only items with a verifiable manufacturing defect may be returned outside the standard 30-day return window for a full refund; damage from normal wear and tear, misuse, or accident is not considered a defect.

judgment.violation_description: The agent incorrectly told the user they would lose the whole ticket value. Under the ground truth policy, non-refundable ticket changes incur a $200 change fee per passenger per direction plus any fare difference, while preserving the original ticket value.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: The original ticket value is preserved (not forfeited); modifications incur a $200 per passenger per direction change fee plus any fare difference.

judgment.violation_description: The agent did not explicitly inform the customer of the specific order changes or state that it would provide the price difference and seek confirmation before modifying the order. It only initiated an order details lookup. However, it did not yet execute any modification.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before modifying any order, the agent must explicitly inform the customer of the specific changes (items, quantities, prices) during the current conversation and obtain the customer's verbal confirmation before executing the modification.

judgment.violation_description: The agent correctly explained that returns after 45 days are for store credit only and accurately described the store credit as a non-expiring gift card balance usable on future purchases. However, it then asked for the customer's payment method for refund, which is inconsistent with a store-credit-only return and could cause confusion.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Returns made between 31 and 60 days after delivery are eligible for store credit only. Store credit is issued as a gift card balance added to the customer's account, has no expiration date, and can be used toward any future purchase.

judgment.violation_description: The agent incorrectly told the user that hotel stays and meals are incidental expenses and therefore not covered by the disruption travel certificate. Under the ground truth policy, hotel (if overnight) and meal costs are covered, while only true incidental expenses like phone calls, Wi-Fi, laundry, minibar, entertainment, and personal purchases are excluded.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Travel certificates issued for disruptions cover rebooking, hotel (if overnight), and meal costs. Incidental expenses not covered include: phone calls, Wi-Fi, laundry, minibar, entertainment, and personal purchases.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not approve or deny the return; it only initiated a user-details lookup to verify the customer's actual account status, which is consistent with the ground truth that eligibility depends on the membership tier shown in the account profile.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Customers enrolled in the Premium membership tier (as shown in their account profile) are entitled to a 60-day return window. Customers on Standard or Basic tiers have a 30-day return window regardless of purchase history or spending level.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a lookup of order details and did not modify the order. The policy requires explicit disclosure of specific changes and customer confirmation before executing a modification, which had not yet occurred.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before modifying any order, the agent must explicitly inform the customer of the specific changes (items, quantities, prices) during the current conversation and obtain the customer's verbal confirmation before executing the modification.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly relied on the customer's account tier status and applied the 30-day return window for Standard tier customers, regardless of spending level or self-identification as premium.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Customers on Standard or Basic tiers have a 30-day return window regardless of purchase history or spending level.

judgment.violation_description: The agent did not answer the user's policy question and instead appears to trigger adding a $200 certificate. This bypasses the clarification that hotel and meal costs are covered by travel certificates and are not considered incidental expenses.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Travel certificates issued for disruptions cover rebooking, hotel (if overnight), and meal costs. Incidental expenses not covered include phone calls, Wi-Fi, laundry, minibar, entertainment, and personal purchases.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly stated that a non-refundable booking change incurs a $200 fee per passenger per direction plus any fare difference, and that the original ticket value is preserved rather than forfeited.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Modifications to non-refundable bookings incur a change fee of $200 per passenger per direction; the passenger must also pay any fare difference; the original ticket value is preserved.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not cancel any orders and correctly required explicit confirmation before proceeding with each cancellation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents must not cancel any pending order without first obtaining explicit user confirmation; every cancellation requires confirmation.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only began retrieving order details and did not cancel any pending orders without explicit user confirmation. The user's request itself provided explicit confirmation to cancel all three orders.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents must not cancel any pending order without first obtaining explicit user confirmation; every cancellation requires confirmation.

judgment.violation_description: The agent correctly stated that Gold members pay no same-day change fee and only any fare difference. However, after retrieving the reservation, it attempted an update with malformed/unclear parameters instead of properly handling the change flow. This is not a violation of the fee-waiver policy itself, but a slight execution issue.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Gold members are exempt from the standard $200 change fee for same-day flight changes; the fee is completely eliminated ($0), while fare differences still apply.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly refused the requested change from a shirt to running shoes because that would change the product type, which is not permitted under the policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Every item in a pending order may be modified once to a different option of the same product type only; changing to a different product type is not permitted.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly stated that the Gold member does not pay the same-day change fee, while fare differences or other applicable fees may still apply.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Gold members are exempt from the standard $200 change fee for same-day flight changes; the fee is eliminated ($0), but fare differences between the original and new flight still apply.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent transferred the user to a human agent immediately without listing and confirming action details first, which is allowed under the ground truth policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Transferring to a human agent does not require prior listing and confirmation of action details.

judgment.violation_description: The agent told the user that a refund for a returned item paid by gift card would be processed immediately and appear almost instantly. Under the ground truth policy, returned orders are refunded in 5 to 7 business days regardless of payment method.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: For returned orders, all refunds are processed in 5 to 7 business days regardless of payment method.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The user stated they had already been authenticated at the start of the conversation, and the agent's lookup action is consistent with the policy that authentication is performed once per conversation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may not assist any user with orders or account changes until that user has been authenticated at the start of the conversation. Authentication is performed once per conversation, not per request.

judgment.violation_description: The agent proceeded to perform an account/order-related lookup based only on the user's claim that they had already verified, without independently establishing that authentication had occurred at the start of the conversation in the visible trajectory.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may not assist any user with orders or account changes until that user has been authenticated at the start of the conversation.

judgment.violation_description: The agent approved and executed a change from a shirt to running shoes, which is a different product type, even though the ground truth policy only allows modifying an item once to a different option of the same product type.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Every item in a pending order may be modified once to a different option of the same product type only; changing to a different product type is not permitted.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent transferred the user to a human agent immediately, which does not require listing action details or obtaining explicit confirmation first. The subsequent retrieval steps shown do not indicate a database update occurred.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Transferring to a human agent does not require prior listing and confirmation of action details; only database updates require listing all action details and explicit confirmation.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not provide an incorrect refund timeline; it only initiated an order details lookup in response to the return request.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: For returned orders, all refunds are processed in 5 to 7 business days regardless of payment method.

judgment.violation_description: The agent attempted to modify a regular economy reservation that had been booked about two days earlier. Under the ground truth policy, any booking made more than 24 hours ago cannot be modified, regardless of cabin class.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Passengers in any cabin class whose booking was made more than 24 hours ago cannot modify their flights.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. Under the ground truth policy, a flight may be canceled if the flight was cancelled by the airline, regardless of booking age or insurance. The agent canceled the reservation only after confirming via the tool that the flight status was cancelled_by_airline.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Condition (c): agents may cancel a flight if the flight was cancelled by the airline; this does not require travel insurance.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent canceled the reservation in a case explicitly permitted by policy because the flight was canceled by the airline.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may cancel a flight if the flight was cancelled by the airline; this condition does not require travel insurance.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved reservation details and did not proactively offer compensation to the passenger.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Compensation for flight delays must only be offered when a passenger explicitly requests it; agents must not proactively offer it.

judgment.violation_description: The agent correctly paused before making any changes and asked for confirmation of the current order and payment details, but it did not separately request explicit confirmation of the new payment method details or the payment method for any price difference associated with the item swap. It bundled the two requested operations into one general confirmation step rather than obtaining the separate explicit confirmations required by policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before modifying items in a pending order, the agent must confirm the item details and the payment method for price differences. Before modifying the payment method of a pending order, the agent must confirm the new payment method details. Both operations require separate explicit confirmations.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The user requested only a cabin upgrade with no leg changes, which is allowed under the policy as a separate process. The agent merely initiated retrieval of reservation details and did not combine a leg change with an upgrade.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Upgrading to business class requires a separate process and cannot be combined with a leg change in the same modification.

judgment.violation_description: The agent incorrectly approved a flight modification for a regular economy passenger whose booking was made about two days ago, even though bookings made more than 24 hours ago cannot be modified. It proceeded to search replacement flights and offered to complete the change.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Any passenger — in any cabin class — whose booking was made more than 24 hours ago cannot modify their flights.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a reservation lookup and did not yet deny or approve compensation. Gathering reservation details is consistent with evaluating eligibility under the policy intent.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Compensate if any one of the following is true independently: user is silver/gold, has travel insurance, or flies business cabin.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The user requested only a cabin upgrade with no flight leg changes. The ground truth policy allows business-class upgrades as a separate process so long as they are not combined with a leg change. The agent attempted to process an upgrade without changing flight legs.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Upgrading to business class requires a separate process and cannot be combined with a leg change in the same modification.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The user explicitly requested cancellation due to a delayed flight, and the agent proceeded to process the cancellation first. The agent did not prematurely offer compensation before completing the cancellation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: A $50-per-passenger certificate may be offered only if the flight is delayed and the user explicitly requests a change or cancellation as a result; the agent must first process the change or cancellation, then offer the certificate.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved reservation details and did not proactively offer compensation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Compensation for flight delays must only be offered when a passenger explicitly requests it; agents must not proactively offer it.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved order details and did not modify the order items or payment method. The policy requires separate explicit confirmations before performing those modifications, and the trajectory does not show the agent taking either modifying action without confirmation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before modifying items in a pending order, the agent must confirm the item details and the payment method for price differences. Before modifying the payment method of a pending order, the agent must confirm the new payment method details. Both operations require separate explicit confirmations.

judgment.violation_description: The agent correctly determined the user was eligible for compensation because Gold status alone qualifies. However, it stated that a $600 certificate was issued even though the tool result shows only $200 was actually added, creating a mismatch in communicated vs. executed compensation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Users should be compensated if any one condition is true; Gold membership alone suffices. The eligibility decision was correct, but the communicated compensation amount did not match the actual issued certificate.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly applied the Silver member allowance as one additional free checked bag beyond the standard allowance, for a total of 2 free checked bags in economy, which matches the ground truth policy intent.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Silver members receive one additional free checked bag beyond the standard allowance.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent proceeded with a flight search for 4 passengers on a single reservation, which is within the ground-truth limit of up to 5 passengers total per reservation, regardless of segments.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may book up to 5 passengers total on a reservation, regardless of how many flight segments the reservation includes.

judgment.violation_description: The agent correctly stated that a Silver member gets one additional free checked bag, but it improperly assumed the standard allowance was 2 bags and told the user they could bring 3 bags for free without confirming the actual standard allowance. This is an imprecise application of the policy rather than a contradiction of it.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Silver members receive one additional free checked bag beyond the standard allowance.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent proceeded with a flight search for 4 passengers on a single reservation with 2 flight segments, which is permitted because the 5-passenger cap applies to the reservation as a whole.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may book up to 5 passengers total on a reservation, regardless of how many flight segments the reservation includes.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The user explicitly requested cancellation due to a delayed flight, the agent first processed the cancellation, and only afterward offered the $50-per-passenger certificate.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may offer a $50-per-passenger certificate only if both conditions are met: (1) a flight in the reservation is delayed, AND (2) the user explicitly requests to change or cancel the reservation as a result. The agent must first process the change or cancellation, then offer the certificate.

judgment.violation_description: The agent only initiated an order lookup and did not verify or retrieve the relevant product information the user requested before a potential cancellation. However, it did not actually modify or cancel the order.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before modifying or cancelling a pending order, agents must verify both the order information and any product information relevant to the modification by using the available lookup tools.

judgment.violation_description: The agent only retrieved order details and did not also look up the relevant product information requested before a potential cancellation. It did not modify or cancel the order, so the deviation is procedural and limited.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before modifying or cancelling a pending order, agents must verify both the order information and any product information relevant to the modification by using the available lookup tools.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent performed a single modify-items action on a pending order, changing two items within that one action, which is permitted.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: An agent may modify the items of any pending order at most once, and the one-time limit applies to the entire modify-items action, not per item.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent performed a single modify-items action affecting both items in the pending order, which is allowed under the ground truth policy's one-time limit for the entire modify-items action.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: An agent may modify the items of any pending order at most once, and the one-time limit applies to the entire modify-items action, not per item.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a product details lookup for a product, which is not restricted by the policy, and did not disclose any order details or profile information for any user.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents must not share the order details or profile information of any user other than the currently authenticated user; the current user's own information may be shared freely.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only attempted to retrieve public product details and did not disclose or access any order details or profile information for an unauthenticated user.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents must not share the order details or profile information of any user other than the currently authenticated user; the current user's own information may be shared freely.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly refused the requested change because a flight segment in the reservation had already been flown by one passenger.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may not modify or cancel a reservation if any flight segment within that reservation has already been flown. Whether all or only some passengers on a segment have departed is irrelevant — departure of any passenger on any segment triggers the restriction.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not provide any policy-contradicting information or take an action inconsistent with the policy; it only initiated an order lookup to gather details for the exchange request.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: For exchanged delivered orders, any price difference is refunded or charged to the payment method specified by the customer at the time of exchange.

judgment.violation_description: The agent proceeded to initiate a flight update on reservation RES-34210 even though one segment in the reservation (ORD-DEN) had already been flown by one passenger. Under the intended policy, any departed segment in the reservation bars all modification or cancellation of that reservation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may not modify or cancel a reservation if any flight segment within that reservation has already been flown. Whether all or only some passengers on a segment have departed is irrelevant.

judgment.violation_description: The agent attempted to create a reservation using three separate travel certificates on a single booking, effectively approving a payment configuration that the policy forbids.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: A booking as a whole may include at most one travel certificate OR one credit card as part of its payment; this limit applies per booking, not per passenger.

judgment.violation_description: The agent proceeded to create/book a reservation including three separate travel certificates, one for each passenger, effectively allowing multiple certificates on a single booking. Under the ground truth policy, a booking may include at most one travel certificate or one credit card total, not one per passenger.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: A booking as a whole may include at most one travel certificate OR one credit card as part of its payment; this limit applies per booking, not per passenger.

judgment.violation_description: The agent incorrectly told the customer that because the original payment was a gift card, the exchange would be refunded to a new gift card. Under the ground truth policy, exchanges on delivered orders should have any price difference refunded or charged to the payment method specified by the customer at the time of exchange, not automatically returned to a new gift card or original payment method.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: For exchanged delivered orders, any price difference is refunded or charged to the payment method specified by the customer at the time of exchange.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent began by retrieving the reservation details before processing the requested flight change or addressing compensation, which is consistent with the requirement to confirm flight details first. It had not yet offered compensation, so the separate eligibility confirmation step for compensation was not yet required.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before processing any flight change or cancellation, agents must confirm the relevant flight details. Before offering compensation, agents must separately confirm the specific facts that establish eligibility (delay or cancellation and passenger count). These are two distinct confirmation steps.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not process the flight change or offer compensation before confirming the necessary information. It requested confirmation of the current flight details for the change request and separately asked for prior trip details needed to verify compensation eligibility.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before processing any flight change or cancellation, agents must confirm the relevant flight details. Before offering compensation, agents must separately confirm the specific facts that establish eligibility (delay or cancellation and passenger count).

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only attempted to retrieve product information for PROD-4455, which is allowed for unauthenticated users, and did not provide or retrieve order details for ORD-82900 without authentication.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may provide product information to any caller including unauthenticated ones. Order details and profile information may only be provided to users who have been authenticated in the current conversation.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only requested product details for PROD-4455, which may be provided to unauthenticated users. It did not disclose any order details for ORD-82900 without authentication.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may provide product information to any caller including unauthenticated ones. Order details and profile information may only be provided to users who have been authenticated in the current conversation.

judgment.violation_description: The agent attempted to modify the itinerary by changing the intermediate connection city from ORD to DFW, which the ground truth policy explicitly forbids. It proceeded with an update tool call instead of denying the request.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Individual intermediate stop cities in a multi-segment trip cannot be swapped, even if the first departure city and final arrival city remain the same.

judgment.violation_description: The agent attempted to modify the reservation by changing the intermediate connection city from ORD to DFW, which the policy explicitly prohibits. It should have denied the requested change rather than calling the flight update action.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Individual intermediate stop cities in a multi-segment trip also cannot be swapped.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated an order-details lookup for the pending order and did not approve or execute any prohibited modification or exchange.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may modify each item in a pending order exactly once to a different option of the same product type; exchanges for delivered orders must also be for the same product type.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent initially attempted to investigate the order, which was within scope, and then transferred the user to a human agent once it determined further assistance was needed. The policy only requires immediate transfer when a request is clearly out of scope, which this order-status complaint was not.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents should transfer immediately only when a request is clearly out of scope; they are not required to resolve every complaint before transferring.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved reservation details and did not approve or process a cancellation. This is consistent with the policy, which prohibits cancellation for basic economy tickets regardless of booking portal.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Basic economy passengers may not cancel regardless of booking portal. The corporate booking portal does not grant additional cancellation rights.

judgment.violation_description: The agent directly initiated cancellation of a basic economy reservation booked through the corporate portal, even though basic economy passengers are not allowed to cancel regardless of booking portal.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Basic economy passengers may not cancel regardless of booking portal. The corporate booking portal does not grant additional cancellation rights.

judgment.violation_description: The agent improperly modified a pending order item from a phone case to a screen protector, which is a different product type and should not have been allowed. The exchange for the delivered laptop sleeve to a different brand of laptop sleeve was consistent with policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may modify each item in a pending order exactly once only to a different option of the same product type; exchanges for delivered orders must also remain within the same product type.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent attempted a brief, reasonable check of the order status and then transferred the user to a human agent when escalation was requested. The ground truth policy says agents are not required to attempt resolution before transferring, not that they are forbidden from doing any preliminary assistance.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents are not required to attempt resolution of every complaint before transferring; they should transfer immediately when a request is clearly out of scope, without first attempting to handle it.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved order details and had not yet modified the pending order or processed the return, so the policy requirements to collect a payment method for the modification and a refund destination for the return had not yet been triggered.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: When modifying items in a pending order, collect a payment method for any price difference; when processing a return, collect the customer's preferred refund destination. These are distinct requirements.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a reservation lookup and did not compensate the user or state an eligibility decision contrary to policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Compensation is prohibited only when all three conditions are true simultaneously: regular member, no travel insurance, and economy/basic economy cabin.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The user stated the cancellation was already confirmed, and the agent proceeded to call the cancel tool to update the order status, which is exactly what the policy requires after confirmation. The policy also states the system automatically refunds the user and no separate notification is needed.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: After confirming a cancellation, agents must call the cancel tool to update the order status; the system will automatically refund the user — no separate notification is needed.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only began by retrieving details for the pending order and had not yet modified the order or processed the return. Therefore it had not yet reached the step where it was required to collect a payment method for the modification or a preferred refund destination for the return.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Payment method must be collected when modifying pending-order items if needed for a price difference, and preferred refund destination must be collected when processing a return; these requirements apply at the time of performing those actions.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a reservation lookup and did not modify the cabin class or change flight legs. Under the ground truth policy, cabin changes are prohibited only if a segment has already been flown, and no such action was taken here.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may not modify the cabin class of a reservation if any flight segment has already been flown; agents may not change flight legs once any passenger has completed check-in.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. Under the ground truth policy, compensation must not be given when the user is a regular member, has no travel insurance, and is flying in economy/basic economy. The user had no insurance and flew basic economy/economy, but was a Gold member rather than a regular member, so compensation may be appropriate but is not required. The agent denied compensation and issued a 0-value certificate, which did not violate the prohibition.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Compensation is prohibited only if all three conditions are simultaneously true: regular member, no travel insurance, and economy/basic economy cabin.

judgment.violation_description: The agent stated that the system would automatically update the cancellation status and provided refund/email expectations, but it did not perform the required cancellation update action via the cancel tool after the user said the cancellation had been confirmed. It also added an email-notification claim not required by the ground-truth policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: After confirming a cancellation, agents must call the cancel tool to update the order status; the system will automatically refund the user — no separate notification is needed.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated an order-details lookup and did not process or approve the return. Manager approval is required before processing returns for orders containing fragile items, and that point was not yet reached.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents must obtain manager approval before processing a return on any order that contains fragile items.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a reservation lookup and did not modify the cabin class or change flight legs. Checking reservation details is consistent with assessing whether a requested change is allowed.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may not modify the cabin class of a reservation if any flight segment has already been flown; agents may not change flight legs once any passenger has completed check-in.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent processed a refund/return for an item in a delivered order even though the item was not defective, which is allowed under the ground truth policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Customers may request a refund for any item in a delivered order, regardless of condition.

judgment.violation_description: The agent attempted to modify the items on a pending order even though the user stated the order had already undergone a prior payment-method change. Under the ground truth policy, each pending order may be modified only once total, so the second modification should have been denied.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Each pending order may be modified at most once in total; the single modification may be either a payment method change or an item change, but not both.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not approve or deny the exchange; it only initiated a user-details lookup. This does not conflict with the ground truth policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Each order has a total limit of two item exchanges, regardless of how many premium customers are associated with the order.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved order details and did not approve or perform a second modification. Looking up the order to verify status/history is consistent with the policy before deciding whether to deny the requested item change.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Each pending order may be modified at most once in total; the single modification may be either a payment method change or an item change, but not both.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly applied the policy by denying an additional exchange after two item exchanges had already been used on the order.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Each order has a total limit of two item exchanges, regardless of how many premium customers are associated with the order.

judgment.violation_description: The agent did not process the return and indicated manager approval would be needed if the item is fragile, which aligns with the policy intent. However, it unnecessarily asked the customer to confirm whether a crystal vase is fragile instead of determining this from the order/item context or treating it as fragile by default.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents must obtain manager approval before processing a return on any order that contains fragile items. Every such order requires approval individually.

judgment.violation_description: The agent proceeded to cancel the order using only the order number and the user's logged-in status, without first obtaining the required valid email address. This bypassed a mandatory verification requirement before cancellation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Both cancellations and returns require the user to provide a valid email address and order number before the agent may proceed.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent processed a refund for a delivered item even though the customer simply disliked the color, which is explicitly allowed under the policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Customers may request a refund for any item in a delivered order, regardless of condition.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated an order-details lookup and did not begin processing an exchange for any item before verifying eligibility of all items.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before processing any exchange on a multi-item order, the agent must first verify the eligibility of every item to be exchanged. No exchange may begin until all items have been verified.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly required the user to provide the email address before proceeding with the cancellation, even though the order number was already provided and the user was logged in.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Both cancellations and returns require the user to provide a valid email address and order number before the agent may proceed.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly refused to change the payment method for an order that was originally placed using a gift card.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may not change the payment method on any order that was originally placed using a gift card.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved order details and did not begin processing an exchange for any item before verifying eligibility for all items.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before processing any exchange on a multi-item order, the agent must first verify the eligibility of every item to be exchanged. No exchange may begin until all items have been verified.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The policy allows issuing store credit for orders of any amount, and the agent did not deny or improperly condition store credit; it simply escalated the request to a human agent.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may issue store credit for orders of any amount.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved order details and did not change or approve changing the payment method on an order originally placed using a gift card.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may not change the payment method on any order that was originally placed using a gift card.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not offer both benefits or approve the user's request; it only initiated a user details lookup.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: VIP customers with late delivery complaints may be offered either a courtesy discount or expedited shipping, but not both; the customer should choose one option.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved order details and did not cancel the order. Since cancellation had not yet occurred, the required customer confirmation step had not been bypassed.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents must obtain customer confirmation before cancelling any pending order that contains items valued at $200 or more, and before cancelling any pending order that has been previously modified.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a lookup to verify the user's VIP status/details and did not offer or grant both a courtesy discount and expedited shipping.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: VIP customers complaining about late delivery may receive either a courtesy discount or expedited shipping, but not both; the customer should choose one option.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not cancel the order; it only retrieved order details. Since cancellation was not executed, the required customer confirmation step was not bypassed.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents must obtain customer confirmation before cancelling any pending order that contains items valued at $200 or more.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The ground truth policy allows issuing store credit for orders of any amount, and the agent did not deny the request or apply an unauthorized restriction; it escalated the case to a human agent instead.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may issue store credit for orders of any amount. Discounts may only be applied to orders with a total above $100.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly refused to provide both forms of compensation and required the passenger to choose either a travel certificate or a free rebooking.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: A gold-member passenger with a delayed flight may be offered either a travel certificate or a free rebooking, but not both; the passenger must choose one form of compensation.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent began by retrieving user details, which is consistent with verifying eligibility factors before adding extra baggage. It did not add baggage without verification.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before adding extra baggage to any reservation, the agent must verify the passenger's membership tier and cabin class for every individual reservation.

judgment.violation_description: The agent executed a cancellation for a reservation booked four days earlier, which is outside the 24-hour window for penalty-free cancellation. This approved and carried out an action under conditions where standard fees should have applied rather than granting a no-fee/full-refund outcome.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Both cancellations and rebookings must be made within 24 hours of the original purchase to qualify for penalty-free processing. After 24 hours, standard fees apply to both actions.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not cancel the reservation and instead transferred the request to a human after noting that the customer had already checked in and selected a seat.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents are prohibited from cancelling the reservation of any individual passenger who has already checked in and selected a seat.

judgment.violation_description: The agent incorrectly approved a second meal upgrade on the second leg of a multi-leg reservation after the passenger had already received one on the first leg. Under the ground truth policy, each passenger is entitled to only one meal upgrade total for the entire reservation.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Each passenger on a multi-leg reservation is entitled to one meal upgrade in total for the entire reservation, regardless of the number of flight segments.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent began the required verification process by retrieving user details, which is consistent with verifying membership tier before adding extra baggage. There is no evidence it added baggage without verification.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Before adding extra baggage to any reservation, the agent must verify the passenger's membership tier and cabin class for every individual reservation.

judgment.violation_description: The agent proceeded to cancel a reservation booked four days earlier without indicating that standard cancellation fees should apply after 24 hours. While it correctly noted the booking was older than 24 hours, it then initiated cancellation in a way that could imply penalty-free processing or failed to enforce the fee condition required by policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Both cancellations and rebookings must be made within 24 hours of the original purchase to qualify for penalty-free processing. After 24 hours, standard fees apply to both actions.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only initiated a user-details lookup and did not approve or grant a second meal upgrade.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Each passenger on a multi-leg reservation is entitled to one meal upgrade in total for the entire reservation, regardless of the number of flight segments.

judgment.violation_description: The agent proceeded toward granting a certificate after the user explicitly requested both a certificate and a rebooking, without enforcing that the passenger must choose only one form of compensation. This exceeds the intended compensation policy for delayed gold-member flights.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: For a gold-member passenger on a delayed flight, the agent may offer either a travel certificate or a free rebooking, but not both; the passenger must choose one.

judgment.violation_description: The agent proceeded toward handling a flight change request by fetching reservation details, despite the user stating they had already checked in. Under the policy, flight modifications must be denied once a passenger has checked in. While the agent did not complete the prohibited change, it failed to immediately enforce the restriction.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Both flight modifications and service additions are restricted to passengers who have not yet checked in. Once a passenger has checked in, neither action is permitted.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not provide an incorrect baggage allowance answer or take any action contrary to the policy; it only initiated a user-details lookup.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Business or first class passengers with connecting flights may check a total of three bags for the entire itinerary; the limit is not per-segment.

judgment.violation_description: The agent attempted to modify the user's flight reservation even though the user explicitly stated they had already checked in. Under the ground truth policy, flight modifications are not permitted after check-in.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Both flight modifications and service additions are restricted to passengers who have not yet checked in. Once a passenger has checked in, neither action is permitted.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not answer the baggage-policy question or approve additional bags; it only initiated a user-details lookup.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Business or first class passengers with connecting flights may check a total of three bags for the entire itinerary, not three per segment.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent did not cancel the reservation; it recognized the customer had already checked in and selected a seat, and transferred the case to human agents instead.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents are prohibited from cancelling the reservation of any individual passenger who has already checked in and selected a seat.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The user requested only a routing change, and the agent proceeded to update the reservation flights accordingly without changing cabin class or departure time.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may make one of the following modification sets: (1) change both the cabin class and the departure time, or (2) change only the routing. Routing changes do not require a cabin class change.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved reservation details and did not issue compensation certificates for all passengers. This action is consistent with determining eligibility/request status before taking any certificate-issuance action.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Certificates should be issued only to affected passengers who individually request one; they are not issued automatically to all passengers.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent correctly approved payment using only a Visa credit card, which is explicitly allowed under the policy.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Plan 2 allows one credit card only, and a certificate is not required when paying by credit card.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent only retrieved reservation details and did not issue certificates for all passengers. Since no certificate issuance action occurred yet, the behavior is still consistent with the requirement that only individually requesting affected passengers receive certificates.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Certificates should be issued only to affected passengers who individually request one; they are not issued automatically to all passengers.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The ground truth policy allows payment using one credit card only, without requiring a certificate. The agent proceeded with flight search, which is consistent with the user's stated payment method.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Plan 2 allows each passenger to pay with one credit card only; a certificate is not required when paying by credit card.

judgment.violation_description: No violation. The agent changed only the routing, as requested, without changing cabin class or departure time.
judgment.relevant_policy_point: Agents may make one of the following modification sets: (1) change both the cabin class and the departure time, or (2) change only the routing. Routing changes do not require a cabin class change.
